title: Day 1 ‚Äî First API & website summarizer
summary: Frontier LLM API ¬∑ `.env` ¬∑ messages ¬∑ scrape ‚Üí summarize pipeline.

sections.heading: Today's goal
sections.remember: Call a **frontier LLM** over the network and build a **website summarizer**: URL ‚Üí scrape ‚Üí prompt ‚Üí markdown summary.

sections.heading: Frontier LLM
sections.remember: Model runs on the **provider's servers** ‚Äî you send prompts via **API key** and pay per use (tokens).
points: Not running weights on your laptop (that's Ollama / HF later).
Jupyter: run cells top-to-bottom with project `.venv` kernel.

sections.heading: Secrets: `.env`
points: Never commit keys ¬∑ no spaces around `=` in `.env`.

sections.heading: Messages format
sections.remember: API expects a **list of dicts**: `role` = `system` | `user` | `assistant`, plus `content`.
points: **System** = rules, tone, format for the whole task.
**User** = your question + data (e.g. scraped website text).

sections.heading: OpenAI client
sections.remember: `OpenAI()` wraps HTTP **`chat.completions.create`** ‚Äî response text is in `choices[0].message.content`.
sections.code:
from openai import OpenAI
openai = OpenAI()
response = openai.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4.1-mini",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}],
)
response.choices[0].message.content

sections.heading: Scraping
sections.remember: `fetch_website_contents(url)` returns plain text (capped ~2000 chars) ‚Äî enough for summaries, not full SPAs.
points: JS-heavy sites may fail ‚Äî need browser automation for production.

sections.heading: End-to-end pipeline
sections.remember: `messages_for(website)` builds system + user prompts ¬∑ `summarize(url)` scrape ‚Üí API ‚Üí return markdown.
points: Use cases: competitor pages, wiki TL;DR, email subject lines.

cheatSheet: system = rules ¬∑ user = task + scraped text
